message:
perf(geometry): add #[inline] to Rect::inner and Rect::union

Rect::inner() is called from 291 locations across 71 files to compute
widget content areas after margins. Rect::union() is similar. Both are
small pure functions that benefit from inlining, matching their siblings
intersection() and intersection_opt() which already have #[inline].

Closes bd-1ijft.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1iqn","title":"Render Budget Enforcement with Graceful Degradation","description":"# Render Budget Enforcement with Graceful Degradation

## Background & Motivation

The plan defines performance budgets (1ms p50 for Present 80x24), but these are **observational** - we measure and regress-test. This bead adds **enforcement** - the system actively prevents budget overruns by degrading gracefully.

**Why this matters for agent harnesses:**
- Agent UIs receive unpredictable content (burst log output, large tool responses)
- A frozen UI during burst input makes the agent feel broken
- Users tolerate reduced visual fidelity; they do NOT tolerate hangs
- Self-regulating performance eliminates the need for manual tuning

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Complements bd-3aqs (output bytes tracking) - that measures, this controls
- Complements bd-19x (benchmarks) - benchmarks detect regressions, this prevents runtime issues
- Extends bd-10i.4.3 (Presenter) - Presenter gains budget awareness

## Technical Design

### Core Types

```rust
/// Render time budget with graceful degradation
pub struct RenderBudget {
    /// Total time budget for this frame
    total: Duration,
    /// When this frame started
    start: Instant,
    /// Current degradation level
    degradation: DegradationLevel,
    /// Per-phase budgets (diff, present, widget-render)
    phase_budgets: PhaseBudgets,
}

/// Progressive degradation levels
#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord)]
pub enum DegradationLevel {
    Full = 0,           // All visual features enabled
    SimpleBorders = 1,  // Unicode box-drawing to ASCII
    NoStyling = 2,      // Colors to monochrome
    EssentialOnly = 3,  // Skip decorative widgets
    Skeleton = 4,       // Just layout boxes
    SkipFrame = 5,      // Emergency: skip frame entirely
}

impl DegradationLevel {
    pub fn next(&self) -> Self {
        match self {
            Self::Full => Self::SimpleBorders,
            Self::SimpleBorders => Self::NoStyling,
            Self::NoStyling => Self::EssentialOnly,
            Self::EssentialOnly => Self::Skeleton,
            Self::Skeleton | Self::SkipFrame => Self::SkipFrame,
        }
    }
}
```

### Budget API

```rust
impl RenderBudget {
    pub fn new(total: Duration) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn remaining(&self) -> Duration {
        self.total.saturating_sub(self.start.elapsed())
    }
    pub fn should_degrade(&self, estimated_cost: Duration) -> bool {
        self.remaining() < estimated_cost
    }
    pub fn degrade(&mut self) { self.degradation = self.degradation.next(); }
    pub fn degradation(&self) -> DegradationLevel { self.degradation }
    pub fn exhausted(&self) -> bool {
        self.remaining().is_zero() || self.degradation == DegradationLevel::SkipFrame
    }
}
```

### Widget Integration

Widgets check degradation level and adjust rendering:

```rust
impl Widget for Panel {
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer, budget: &RenderBudget) {
        match budget.degradation() {
            DegradationLevel::Full => {
                self.render_unicode_border(area, buf);
                self.render_title_styled(area, buf);
            }
            DegradationLevel::SimpleBorders => {
                self.render_ascii_border(area, buf);  // +- instead of box chars
                self.render_title_styled(area, buf);
            }
            DegradationLevel::NoStyling => {
                self.render_ascii_border(area, buf);
                self.render_title_plain(area, buf);   // No colors
            }
            DegradationLevel::EssentialOnly => {
                self.render_content_only(area, buf);
            }
            DegradationLevel::Skeleton => {
                buf.fill_rect(area, Cell::new(' '));
            }
            DegradationLevel::SkipFrame => {}
        }
    }
}
```

### Recovery Mechanism

```rust
impl RenderBudget {
    pub fn should_upgrade(&self) -> bool {
        // If >50% budget remaining, try upgrading next frame
        self.remaining() > self.total / 2 && self.degradation != DegradationLevel::Full
    }
    pub fn upgrade(&mut self) {
        self.degradation = match self.degradation {
            DegradationLevel::SkipFrame => DegradationLevel::Skeleton,
            DegradationLevel::Skeleton => DegradationLevel::EssentialOnly,
            DegradationLevel::EssentialOnly => DegradationLevel::NoStyling,
            DegradationLevel::NoStyling => DegradationLevel::SimpleBorders,
            _ => DegradationLevel::Full,
        };
    }
}
```

### Configuration

```rust
pub struct FrameBudgetConfig {
    pub total: Duration,           // default: 16ms (~60fps feel)
    pub diff_budget: Duration,     // default: 2ms
    pub present_budget: Duration,  // default: 4ms
    pub allow_frame_skip: bool,    // default: true
    pub degradation_cooldown: u32, // frames between level changes
}
```

## Test Plan

### Unit Tests
- [ ] RenderBudget::remaining() correctly computes time left
- [ ] DegradationLevel ordering is correct (Full < SkipFrame)
- [ ] should_degrade() returns true when cost > remaining
- [ ] Upgrade/downgrade cycle is symmetric and bounded
- [ ] Default config values are sensible

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Widget renders at all degradation levels without panic
- [ ] Frame loop respects budget.exhausted()
- [ ] Recovery mechanism restores full fidelity after burst

### Property Tests
- [ ] Degradation level never exceeds SkipFrame
- [ ] Budget remaining is always >= 0
- [ ] With infinite budget, degradation stays at Full

### E2E Tests
```bash
./ftui-harness --test-mode burst-logs --log-rate 10000/s --duration 5s
# Assert: no frames take > 2x budget
# Assert: UI remains responsive (input latency < 100ms)
```

## Logging Requirements

All degradation events logged at WARN level with frame number, from/to levels, and trigger phase.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] RenderBudget type implemented in ftui-render
- [ ] DegradationLevel enum with 6 levels
- [ ] All harness-essential widgets respect degradation
- [ ] Frame loop integrates budget checking
- [ ] Recovery mechanism prevents permanent degradation
- [ ] Unit tests pass with 100% coverage of public API
- [ ] E2E burst test shows no >2x budget overruns","notes":"## Missing API: Frame Reset

The RenderBudget needs a method to reset for new frames:

```rust
impl RenderBudget {
    /// Reset budget for a new frame
    /// Called at the start of each render cycle
    pub fn new_frame(&mut self) {
        self.start = Instant::now();
        // Don't reset degradation - let it recover naturally via should_upgrade()
    }
    
    /// Force reset to full quality (for testing or user override)
    pub fn reset_to_full(&mut self) {
        self.degradation = DegradationLevel::Full;
        self.start = Instant::now();
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-1un","title":"Document Terminal Compatibility Matrix","description":"# Terminal Compatibility Matrix Documentation

## Background
Plan Chapter 16 provides a detailed compatibility matrix. This needs to be documented for users to understand what features work where.

## Compatibility Matrix (from Plan)

| Feature | Kitty | WezTerm | Alacritty | Ghostty | iTerm2 | GNOME Term | Win Term |
|---------|-------|---------|-----------|---------|--------|------------|----------|
| True Color | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Sync Output | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
| OSC 8 Links | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ |
| Kitty Keyboard | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
| Kitty Graphics | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
| Sixel | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ |
| Focus Events | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Bracketed Paste | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |

## Feature Details

### True Color (24-bit RGB)
- Detection: COLORTERM=truecolor or COLORTERM=24bit
- Fallback: 256-color or 16-color palette
- Most modern terminals support this

### Synchronized Output (DEC 2026)
- Purpose: Atomic frame updates, flicker prevention
- Detection: Known terminal identification
- Fallback: Buffered writes still help
- ftui activates when detected

### OSC 8 Hyperlinks
- Purpose: Clickable links in terminal
- Format: `\x1b]8;;URL\x1b\\text\x1b]8;;\x1b\\`
- Windows Terminal: Not supported
- Most Linux/macOS terminals support

### Kitty Keyboard Protocol
- Purpose: Enhanced key event information
- Provides: press/repeat/release, modifiers
- Limited adoption outside Kitty ecosystem
- Feature-gated in ftui

### Scroll Region (DECSTBM)
- Purpose: Inline mode optimization
- Support: Nearly universal
- Quirks: tmux/screen passthrough issues
- ftui uses as optimization, not requirement

## Multiplexer Notes

### tmux
- Passthrough: Requires `\x1bPtmux;...\x1b\\`
- Sync output: Blocked by default
- OSC 8: Needs recent version + config
- Detection: TMUX env var

### screen
- Limited modern feature support
- No sync output passthrough
- Basic ANSI works fine
- Detection: STY env var

### Zellij
- Better passthrough than tmux/screen
- Native terminal support varies
- Detection: ZELLIJ env var

## Windows Considerations

### Windows Terminal
- True color: ✓
- Mouse: ✓
- OSC 8: ✗
- Sync output: ✗
- Raw mode: Via Crossterm

### ConEmu/Cmder
- Limited feature set
- True color: ✓ (newer versions)
- ANSI support varies by version

### cmd.exe / PowerShell (legacy)
- Very limited ANSI support
- Windows 10+ improved support
- Use Windows Terminal for best experience

## Inline Mode Notes
- Works everywhere (no special requirements)
- Cursor save/restore: Universal
- Scroll region: Nearly universal (optimization only)
- Sync output: Nice-to-have (not required)

## Recommended Test Matrix
For CI, test against:
1. Kitty (most features)
2. WezTerm (cross-platform)
3. Alacritty (popular, minimal)
4. Plain Linux VT (baseline)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Matrix table documented
- [ ] Feature descriptions complete
- [ ] Multiplexer notes included
- [ ] Windows section clear
- [ ] Inline mode notes present
- [ ] Test matrix recommended

 {"id":"bd-1vb18","title":"Fix emoji-triggered border corruption in demo showcase (keep emojis)","description":"Problem
- Demo showcase has border misalignment/corruption in File Browser and Markdown screens when emojis are present. Emojis must remain.

Likely Causes
1) Several screens try to "clear" the full area using Paragraph::new("") + bg style, but Paragraph only paints style and does not clear cell content. With Flex gaps between panels, stale border characters from prior frames/screens can bleed into those gaps and appear as misaligned borders.
2) Buffer::print_text_clipped (ftui-render) advances by grapheme_width() but may only write the first char for multi-codepoint graphemes (e.g. VS16 sequences like "⚙️"), leaving holes that can preserve stale border chars.

Fix
- Clear demo screen root areas with actual space cells (bg=surface) so gaps are deterministically blank.
- Make Buffer::print_text_clipped fill any extra width when falling back to first char so no holes remain (and add a regression test).

Acceptance
- File Browser and Markdown screens render without stray/misaligned borders in gaps.
- Emojis remain enabled in file browser icons and unicode showcase.
- cargo fmt/check/clippy/test pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GrayFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-06T02:01:09.704771751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T02:34:21.706728130Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T02:34:21.706701631Z","close_reason":"Completed (emoji border corruption fixed: VS16 width policy + buffer filler + empty paragraph clears)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["borders","demo-showcase","emoji","render","text"]}

 {"id":"bd-2lwf","title":"Time-Travel Debugging with Frame Snapshots","description":"# Time-Travel Debugging with Frame Snapshots

## Background & Motivation

Visual bugs are notoriously hard to debug. By the time you notice something wrong, the state that caused it is gone. Traditional logging captures events but not visual state.

**Why this matters:**
- Layout bugs manifest visually, not in logs
- Race conditions cause intermittent visual glitches
- Users report "I saw X but now it shows Y" - impossible to repro
- Debugging requires exact visual state at bug time

**The solution:**
- Record frame snapshots (compressed) during development
- Enable "rewind" to inspect past visual states
- Export session recordings for bug reports
- Integrate with ProgramSimulator for deterministic replay

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Extends bd-10i.8.3 (ProgramSimulator) with visual snapshots
- Enhances bd-2u4 (snapshot/golden testing) with runtime recording

## Technical Design

### Core Types

```rust
/// Time-travel recording session
pub struct TimeTravel {
    /// Circular buffer of frame snapshots
    snapshots: VecDeque<CompressedFrame>,
    /// Maximum snapshots to keep
    capacity: usize,
    /// Frame counter
    frame_number: u64,
    /// Whether recording is active
    recording: bool,
    /// Metadata for each frame
    metadata: VecDeque<FrameMetadata>,
}

/// Compressed frame (delta-encoded from previous)
pub struct CompressedFrame {
    /// Base frame number (for delta)
    base: Option<u64>,
    /// Changed cells (sparse)
    changes: Vec<CellChange>,
    /// Checksum for integrity
    checksum: u32,
}

pub struct CellChange {
    x: u16,
    y: u16,
    cell: Cell,
}

/// Metadata attached to each frame
pub struct FrameMetadata {
    /// Frame number
    frame: u64,
    /// Timestamp
    timestamp: Instant,
    /// Events that triggered this frame
    events: Vec<Event>,
    /// Model hash (for identifying state)
    model_hash: u64,
    /// Render duration
    render_time: Duration,
}
```

### Recording API

```rust
impl TimeTravel {
    /// Create a new recorder with given capacity
    pub fn new(capacity: usize) -> Self {
        Self {
            snapshots: VecDeque::with_capacity(capacity),
            capacity,
            frame_number: 0,
            recording: true,
            metadata: VecDeque::with_capacity(capacity),
        }
    }
    
    /// Record a frame (call from runtime after each present)
    pub fn record(&mut self, frame: &Frame, events: &[Event], render_time: Duration) {
        if \!self.recording {
            return;
        }
        
        let compressed = if let Some(prev) = self.snapshots.back() {
            // Delta-encode from previous
            CompressedFrame::delta(frame, prev)
        } else {
            // First frame: full snapshot
            CompressedFrame::full(frame)
        };
        
        // Evict oldest if at capacity
        if self.snapshots.len() >= self.capacity {
            self.snapshots.pop_front();
            self.metadata.pop_front();
        }
        
        let metadata = FrameMetadata {
            frame: self.frame_number,
            timestamp: Instant::now(),
            events: events.to_vec(),
            model_hash: frame.model_hash(),
            render_time,
        };
        
        self.snapshots.push_back(compressed);
        self.metadata.push_back(metadata);
        self.frame_number += 1;
    }
    
    /// Get current recording size
    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {
        self.snapshots.len()
    }
    
    /// Pause/resume recording
    pub fn set_recording(&mut self, recording: bool) {
        self.recording = recording;
    }
}
```

### Replay API

```rust
impl TimeTravel {
    /// Get frame at specific index (0 = oldest recorded)
    pub fn get(&self, index: usize) -> Option<Frame> {
        if index >= self.snapshots.len() {
            return None;
        }
        
        // Reconstruct frame by applying deltas
        let mut frame = Frame::default();
        for i in 0..=index {
            self.snapshots[i].apply_to(&mut frame);
        }
        Some(frame)
    }
    
    /// Get frame N steps back from current
    pub fn rewind(&self, steps: usize) -> Option<Frame> {
        let index = self.snapshots.len().checked_sub(steps + 1)?;
        self.get(index)
    }
    
    /// Get metadata for frame
    pub fn metadata(&self, index: usize) -> Option<&FrameMetadata> {
        self.metadata.get(index)
    }
    
    /// Find frame by model hash
    pub fn find_by_hash(&self, hash: u64) -> Option<usize> {
        self.metadata.iter().position(|m| m.model_hash == hash)
    }
    
    /// Get all frames between two points
    pub fn range(&self, start: usize, end: usize) -> Vec<Frame> {
        (start..end.min(self.snapshots.len()))
            .filter_map(|i| self.get(i))
            .collect()
    }
}
```

### Compression Strategy

```rust
impl CompressedFrame {
    /// Create delta-encoded frame
    pub fn delta(current: &Frame, previous: &CompressedFrame) -> Self {
        let prev_frame = previous.decompress();
        let mut changes = Vec::new();
        
        for y in 0..current.height() {
            for x in 0..current.width() {
                let curr_cell = current.get(x, y);
                let prev_cell = prev_frame.get(x, y);
                
                if \!curr_cell.bits_eq(prev_cell) {
                    changes.push(CellChange { x, y, cell: *curr_cell });
                }
            }
        }
        
        Self {
            base: Some(previous.frame_number()),
            changes,
            checksum: current.checksum(),
        }
    }
    
    /// Apply changes to reconstruct frame
    pub fn apply_to(&self, frame: &mut Frame) {
        for change in &self.changes {
            frame.set(change.x, change.y, change.cell);
        }
    }
    
    /// Estimated memory usage
    pub fn memory_size(&self) -> usize {
        std::mem::size_of::<Self>() + 
        self.changes.len() * std::mem::size_of::<CellChange>()
    }
}
```

### Export/Import

```rust
impl TimeTravel {
    /// Export recording to file (for bug reports)
    pub fn export(&self, path: &Path) -> io::Result<()> {
        let file = File::create(path)?;
        let mut writer = GzEncoder::new(file, Compression::default());
        
        // Write header
        writer.write_all(b"FTUI-TTRAVEL-V1\\n")?;
        
        // Write frames
        for (snapshot, metadata) in self.snapshots.iter().zip(self.metadata.iter()) {
            bincode::serialize_into(&mut writer, &(snapshot, metadata))?;
        }
        
        Ok(())
    }
    
    /// Import recording from file
    pub fn import(path: &Path) -> io::Result<Self> {
        let file = File::open(path)?;
        let mut reader = GzDecoder::new(file);
        
        // Read and validate header
        let mut header = [0u8; 16];
        reader.read_exact(&mut header)?;
        if \!header.starts_with(b"FTUI-TTRAVEL-V1") {
            return Err(io::Error::new(io::ErrorKind::InvalidData, "Invalid header"));
        }
        
        // Read frames
        let mut time_travel = Self::new(1000);
        while let Ok((snapshot, metadata)) = bincode::deserialize_from(&mut reader) {
            time_travel.snapshots.push_back(snapshot);
            time_travel.metadata.push_back(metadata);
        }
        
        Ok(time_travel)
    }
}
```

### Debug UI Integration

```rust
/// Overlay widget for time-travel inspection
pub struct TimeTravelInspector {
    time_travel: Arc<RwLock<TimeTravel>>,
    current_index: usize,
    playing: bool,
    playback_speed: f32,
}

impl TimeTravelInspector {
    pub fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        let tt = self.time_travel.read().unwrap();
        
        // Render timeline bar
        let timeline_area = Rect::new(area.x, area.bottom() - 1, area.width, 1);
        self.render_timeline(&tt, timeline_area, buf);
        
        // Render frame preview
        if let Some(frame) = tt.get(self.current_index) {
            let preview_area = Rect::new(area.x, area.y, area.width, area.height - 2);
            frame.render(preview_area, buf);
        }
        
        // Render metadata
        if let Some(meta) = tt.metadata(self.current_index) {
            let info = format\!(
                "Frame {} | Events: {} | Render: {:?}",
                meta.frame,
                meta.events.len(),
                meta.render_time
            );
            buf.set_string(area.x, area.bottom() - 2, &info, Style::default());
        }
    }
    
    pub fn handle_event(&mut self, event: Event) {
        match event {
            Event::Key(KeyCode::Left, _) => self.step_back(),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::Right, _) => self.step_forward(),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::Space, _) => self.toggle_playback(),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::Home, _) => self.jump_to_start(),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::End, _) => self.jump_to_end(),
            _ => {}
        }
    }
}
```

- [ ] All tests pass","notes":"## Thread Safety Note

The TimeTravelInspector uses Arc<RwLock<TimeTravel>>. Current implementation uses unwrap():

```rust
let tt = self.time_travel.read().unwrap();
```

**Issue:** This can panic if the lock is poisoned (another thread panicked while holding it).

**Fix:** Use expect() with context or handle the error:

```rust
// Option 1: expect with context
let tt = self.time_travel.read()
    .expect("TimeTravel lock poisoned");

// Option 2: Handle gracefully
let tt = match self.time_travel.read() {
    Ok(guard) => guard,
    Err(poisoned) => {
        tracing::warn!("TimeTravel lock was poisoned, recovering");
        poisoned.into_inner()
    }
};
```

The second approach allows recovery from poisoned locks, which is more robust for debugging tools.

 {"id":"bd-2m5","title":"SIMD-accelerated cell comparison and buffer operations","description":"# SIMD/autovec accelerated hot paths (NO unsafe)

## Objective
Improve hot-path performance (cell comparisons, clears, copies, ASCII width checks) **without introducing unsafe code**.

Project constraint: `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` across our crates. We must not create an “unsafe-only SIMD crate”.

## Plan Alignment
The plan mentions optional SIMD acceleration. In this repo, that must translate into one of:
- compiler autovectorization via carefully-written scalar loops
- safe portable SIMD (only if available without `unsafe` in our Rust toolchain)
- leveraging well-vetted dependencies that encapsulate unsafe internally (our crate remains `forbid(unsafe_code)`)

## Scope
Target the following operations, in priority order:
1. Cell equality comparisons in diff scan
2. Buffer clear/fill
3. Row/rect copies
4. ASCII-width fast paths

## Strategy
- Write scalar loops that are friendly to autovec:
  - contiguous slices
  - minimal branching
  - row-major iteration
- Use benchmark-driven iteration:
  - measure p50/p95 for 80x24 and larger terminals
  - confirm no regressions in correctness tests

## Explicit Non-Goal
- Adding `unsafe` blocks or `#[target_feature]` functions inside ftui code.

If later we decide explicit unsafe SIMD is required, that needs an explicit policy change (and likely an ADR + safety review).

## Tests Required
- Property tests confirming optimized paths exactly match the reference scalar behavior.
- Benchmarks that show improvements and include regression budgets.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Optimizations preserve correctness (property tests pass; byte-for-byte identical results where applicable).
- [ ] Benchmarks demonstrate measurable improvements on at least one key hot path.
- [ ] No unsafe code is introduced in ftui crates.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:20:50.805814276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T19:51:43.104701371Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T19:51:43.104678769Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2m5","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2mc1","title":"Semantic Event System: High-Level Input Abstraction","description":"## Background

FrankenTUI has excellent low-level input parsing in `ftui-core/src/input_parser.rs` that transforms raw terminal bytes into `Event` types (Key, Mouse, Resize, etc.). However, **every widget must implement its own gesture detection** for common patterns like:

- Double-click detection (file browser, list selection)
- Long-press recognition (context menus)
- Drag operations (text selection, list reordering)
- Chord sequences (Ctrl+K Ctrl+C style shortcuts)
- Swipe gestures (touch terminal support)

This leads to **duplicated, inconsistent, and often missing** gesture handling across widgets.

## Problem Statement

Consider implementing double-click in a List widget:
1. Track last click time and position
2. Define timeout threshold (300ms typical)
3. Handle edge cases (different positions, mouse up/down)
4. This code is repeated in Table, FilePicker, TreeView...

The same applies to drag detection, long-press, and chords.

## Solution: Semantic Event Layer

Add a `GestureRecognizer` in `ftui-runtime` that:
1. Receives raw `Event` from terminal
2. Maintains state (click history, mouse position, key sequences)
3. Emits `SemanticEvent` when gestures are recognized
4. Widgets receive high-level events instead of raw input

## Proposed API

```rust
// New event types in ftui-core
pub enum SemanticEvent {
    // Mouse gestures
    Click { pos: Position, button: MouseButton },
    DoubleClick { pos: Position, button: MouseButton },
    TripleClick { pos: Position, button: MouseButton }, // Line selection
    LongPress { pos: Position, duration: Duration },
    
    // Drag gestures
    DragStart { pos: Position, button: MouseButton },
    DragMove { from: Position, to: Position, delta: (i16, i16) },
    DragEnd { from: Position, to: Position },
    DragCancel,
    
    // Keyboard gestures  
    Chord { sequence: Vec<KeyEvent> }, // e.g., Ctrl+K Ctrl+C
    
    // Touch-like gestures (for touch terminals)
    Swipe { direction: Direction, distance: u16, velocity: f32 },
    Pinch { scale: f32 }, // Future: zoom
}

// Recognizer configuration
pub struct GestureConfig {
    pub double_click_timeout: Duration,  // Default: 300ms
    pub long_press_threshold: Duration,  // Default: 500ms
    pub drag_threshold: u16,             // Default: 3 cells
    pub chord_timeout: Duration,         // Default: 1000ms
    pub enable_swipe: bool,              // Default: false
}

// Stateful recognizer
pub struct GestureRecognizer {
    config: GestureConfig,
    // Internal state
    last_click: Option<(Instant, Position, MouseButton)>,
    drag_state: Option<DragState>,
    chord_buffer: Vec<KeyEvent>,
    chord_start: Option<Instant>,
}

impl GestureRecognizer {
    pub fn process(&mut self, event: Event) -> Vec<SemanticEvent>;
    pub fn reset(&mut self);
    pub fn is_dragging(&self) -> bool;
}
```

## Integration with Runtime

```rust
// In Program::run()
loop {
    let raw_event = terminal.read_event()?;
    
    // Process through gesture recognizer
    let semantic_events = self.gesture_recognizer.process(raw_event.clone());
    
    // Model receives both raw and semantic events
    for semantic in semantic_events {
        let msg = Message::Semantic(semantic);
        self.model.update(msg);
    }
    
    // Raw event still available if needed
    let msg = Message::from(raw_event);
    self.model.update(msg);
}
```

## Benefits

1. **Consistency**: All widgets get the same gesture detection
2. **Correctness**: Edge cases handled once, correctly
3. **Configurability**: Users can tune thresholds
4. **Extensibility**: New gestures added in one place
5. **Testing**: Gesture recognition is unit-testable

## Performance Considerations

- GestureRecognizer maintains minimal state (~100 bytes)
- Processing is O(1) per event
- No allocations on hot path (events returned via SmallVec)
- Configurable to disable unused gesture types

## Files to Create/Modify

- crates/ftui-core/src/semantic_event.rs (NEW)
- crates/ftui-core/src/gesture.rs (NEW)
- crates/ftui-runtime/src/program.rs (integrate)
- crates/ftui-core/src/lib.rs (exports)

## Success Metrics

- Zero duplicated gesture detection in widgets
- All existing widgets migrated to semantic events
- Gesture recognition tests cover edge cases
- No performance regression in event processing

## Success Criteria
- Epic delivers a cohesive user-visible capability: Semantic Event System: High-Level Input Abstraction
- Child tasks are not just implemented; each has unit tests + E2E scripts with verbose JSONL logs and deterministic fixtures
- Graceful degradation tiers are specified (capabilities, fallbacks, safety) and validated in tests
- Formal invariants documented + enforced via property tests; any dynamic decisions have an evidence ledger and debug UI


## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.
- Metamorphic tests: shrink/grow, reorder non-semantic inputs, and ensure invariants still hold.

 {"id":"bd-jbg","title":"Implement forms and picker widgets","description":"PHASE 7 - EXTRAS: FORMS AND PICKERS

## Background
Interactive forms and picker dialogs are common UI patterns for configuration, data entry, and selection. Feature-gated as optional extra.

## Technical Requirements

### Form Widget
- Field types: TextInput, Checkbox, Radio, Select, Number
- Validation support with error messages
- Tab navigation between fields
- Submit/Cancel actions
- Vertical or horizontal layout
- Required field indicators

### Form API

pub struct Form {
    fields: Vec<FormField>,
    focused: usize,
    on_submit: Option<Box<dyn Fn(&FormData)>>,
}

pub enum FormField {
    Text { label: String, value: String, placeholder: Option<String> },
    Checkbox { label: String, checked: bool },
    Radio { label: String, options: Vec<String>, selected: usize },
    Select { label: String, options: Vec<String>, selected: usize },
    Number { label: String, value: i64, min: Option<i64>, max: Option<i64> },
}

impl Form {
    pub fn new(fields: Vec<FormField>) -> Self;
    pub fn focus_next(&mut self);
    pub fn focus_prev(&mut self);
    pub fn submit(&self) -> FormData;
    pub fn validate(&self) -> Vec<ValidationError>;
}

### Picker Widgets
- FilePicker: Browse filesystem with filtering
- ColorPicker: Visual color selection
- DatePicker: Calendar-based date selection
- ConfirmDialog: Yes/No confirmation

### Picker API

pub struct FilePicker {
    current_dir: PathBuf,
    filter: Option<String>,  // glob pattern
    show_hidden: bool,
    selection: Option<PathBuf>,
}

impl FilePicker {
    pub fn new(start_dir: impl AsRef<Path>) -> Self;
    pub fn filter(mut self, pattern: &str) -> Self;
    pub fn show_hidden(mut self, show: bool) -> Self;
    pub fn selected(&self) -> Option<&Path>;
}

## Tests Required
- [ ] Form field navigation works
- [ ] Validation errors display correctly
- [ ] Submit collects all values
- [ ] FilePicker navigates directories
- [ ] ColorPicker returns valid RGB

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All form field types implemented
- [ ] Keyboard navigation complete
- [ ] FilePicker with glob filtering
- [ ] ConfirmDialog with Yes/No
- [ ] Accessible focus indicators
